entry.title: Re: Implemented OAuth2 Policy in APIM Integration suite s...
entry.content:
Remove Header Authorization before reading CPI service key details and passing . Looks like APIM header auth is passing to CPI.

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="yes"?>
<AssignMessage async="false" continueOnError="false" enabled="false" xmlns="http://www.sap.com/apimgmt">
    <Remove>
        <Headers>
            <Header name="Authorization"/>
        </Headers>
    </Remove>
    <IgnoreUnresolvedVariables>true</IgnoreUnresolvedVariables>
    <AssignTo createNew="false" transport="http" type="request"></AssignTo>
</AssignMessage>

 

 

After this you can pass CPI service key details

entry.title: MATERIALNUMBER difference between POSDW/MON0 and TLOGF table in CAR POSDTA
entry.content:
As per our requirement we have multiple Article assigned against One EAN code. Now, each SKU is assigned to different Sales Area. To restrict correct Article being pulled into CARPOSDTA. We have enhanced, Masterdata Enrichment Task and got Article code updated in TLOGF table by validating MVKE table entry. 

unfortunately, the workbench (POSDW/MON0) shows us the wrong Article code. Question is, How to resolve this Issue?

entry.title: SAP TechEd 2025: Registration is live – check out our brand new hybrid experience!
entry.content:
Let's take a stroll down memory lane, shall we? SAP TechEd has been with us for a long time. It's part of our DNA. It's the fabric of our community. We started small: 1 event, 1 location. Then we expanded a bit: 1 event, 3 locations. Then came the pandemic (sigh), and changing times called for changing strategies. We went completely virtual: global, accessible, flexible. We met our audience where they were. Then came (yet another) shift in the paradigm – in-person events came back, but we still wanted to reach as many SAP fans as possible, so we created 2 events: in-person and virtual. Whew…that’s a lotta change!

Register here for TechEd!  

SAP TechEd goes Hybrid

This year, we are combining the best of both worlds and launching our brand-new hybrid experience for SAP TechEd in 2025. What exactly does that mean, you may ask?

It means that a full-fledged, in-person, old school SAP TechEd is back – taking place in Berlin November 4–6 at the Messe Berlin. Yep, you read that correctly. We couldn’t be more excited! So, what exactly can you expect? You name it – Strategy Talks, Road Map sessions, Deep Dives, Hands-on Workshops, Demo Stations and a full-blown Community Clubhouse with fan favorites like the Developer Garage and the Community Theater. Berlin is your place to connect with SAP experts, the Developer Advocates and each other IN THE FLESH! TechEd fans rejoice! No more, "Sorry, I was muted" or "Oops, could you repeat that, my connection is a bit wonky today." We’re talking real people, in a real place, with a real goal – learning and networking. To quote one of our mentors, TechEd Berlin promises to be "an SAP Community family reunion."

What about “hybrid”? How do the rest of us get in on the fun?

Let me explain. We'll be LIVE streaming select sessions from Berlin for our live virtual show. The sessions will be recorded and available on-demand as well. Real Berlin sessions – live or on-demand. AND we'll have a hybrid Q&A experience - meaning our experts will be taking questions from both our live and virtual audiences. Make sense? Wait – it gets better. We'll also have exclusive virtual segments hosted from our LIVE STUDIO right in the middle of the action on the SAP TechEd show floor in Berlin. Our roving reporters will give you a peek at what's happening throughout the venue, so our extended global community can feel like they are part of the action, right there with us. 🙂

Tell me more! What can I learn about @SAPTechEd?

I’m so happy you asked! 😉We have NINE tracks covering the most important technology topics our community needs to and wants to know about:

  • SAP Business Technology Platform – General

Dive into SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) to learn about our strategy and to dig deeper into topics that are important for your day-to-day operations on SAP BTP. You can learn how to streamline your DevOps workflows, implement security best practices, and follow strategic architecture recommendations from SAP experts. Stay ahead of the curve with the latest SAP BTP road-map information, empowering you with the knowledge and resources to drive the future of your organization.

  • Application Development and Automation

SAP Build solutions, including the ABAP Cloud model, provides what you need to build, extend, and automate applications across your landscape. From back-end services to the SAP Fiori user experience, workspaces, automations, and AI agents, you gain offerings empowered by the Joule copilot and designed to keep your core clean. Discover the latest innovations in SAP Build, explore how to develop and extend applications and processes, and learn how to create user experiences and Joule skills tailored to the way you work.

  • Integration

To ensure operational success, organizations must seamlessly integrate and automate business processes, APIs, and events across any application landscape. SAP Integration Suite empowers developers to drive innovation with an integration platform as a service (iPaaS) on SAP Business Technology Platform, which unifies on-premises and cloud-based systems, applications, events, and data. With support for event-driven architecture, the suite enables real-time responsiveness and agility by allowing systems to react instantly to business events. Discover how AI-powered capabilities in SAP Integration Suite can help harmonize user experiences and improve developer productivity and operational excellence across complex and diverse IT landscapes.

  • Data and Analytics

The future belongs to data-driven organizations. To achieve true digital transformation, organizations must access insights wherever their data resides, independent from shape or form. With data landscapes distributed among different applications and systems both in the cloud and on premises, organizations spend countless resources extracting, rebuilding, and integrating their data for their use cases. With data and analytics solutions running on SAP Business Technology Platform, organizations can build intelligent solutions, transform planning with a business data fabric, and ground and train AI with trusted data.

  • Cloud Applications

Immerse yourself in the critical suite qualities that help ensure your applications and solutions shine brightly: user experience, design, mobile apps, security, and compliance. Stay ahead with the latest trends, tools, and best practices in UX/UI design, mobile development, security protocols, and compliance frameworks. Equip yourself with the knowledge and skills to develop more secure, compliant, and user-centric solutions that not only meet but also exceed user expectations. Explore how these critical components can transform your applications into shining stars in your industry.

  • Digital Transformation with Cloud ERP

Cloud ERP supports your digital transformation with solutions for your needs. Whether you’re adopting your first ERP or migrating from an existing one, learn how Cloud ERP offers a comprehensive, modular, and extensible solution driven by built-in AI and analytics. Experience how to maximize the business value from Cloud ERP with innovative tools and AI-driven accelerators, and learn how to extend the software to create distinctive value for your organization.

  • Business AI and Joule

Make rapid, transformative business outcomes a reality, and rethink how business is done in the age of AI, accelerating your business transformation. With the Joule copilot becoming the new UI and Joule Agents reimagining how tasks are executed, we are transforming business processes from the ground up. And all of this is empowered by SAP’s unified AI operating system, which equips developers with a complement of tools and technologies to build, extend, and run custom AI solutions and agents at scale—accessible through one single entry point.

  • Business Transformation Management

Navigate constant change with confidence by turning business transformation into a core capability. Discover how to unify enterprise architecture, business process management, and digital adoption with the latest innovations from SAP LeanIX, SAP Signavio, and WalkMe solutions. Explore agentic AI and learn how to build agents that surface insights, drive innovation, and boost efficiency. Then apply these tools to optimize processes, modernize IT, and realize business value.

  • Customer Support and Cloud Lifecycle Management

Embrace the full power of Customer Support & Cloud Lifecycle Management at SAP. Dive into the cutting-edge SAP Cloud ALM solution for application lifecycle management, master transformation tooling for the RISE with SAP and GROW with SAP journeys, and see how AI is redefining the support experience. Whether you’re building clean core landscapes, automating operations, or driving uptime with intelligence, you can gain the skills to lead with confidence and innovate with purpose.

Sounds cool! But break it down for me. How will these topics come to life?

Great question. We'll have different content formats so you can choose how you want to learn and interact. Do you like to sit back and watch the experts? Or do you prefer to get your hands "dirty" with some coding? Do you like getting insights and overviews or do you want to dive deep into a particular topic?

SAP TechEd is about so much more... and trust me – we have so much more in store for you:

So, what's the takeaway? What's TechEd 2025 all about? 

It's about... LEARNING

It's about... CONNECTING

It's about... COMMUNITY

It's... SAP TechEd

Register today and let me know in the comments what you are looking forward to the most! I can’t WAIT to share it with all of you!

Register here for TechEd!

entry.title: Re: How to capture the message content in a Exception sub...
entry.content:
Try below

  • error/innererror/errordetails/errordetail/message/text()
  • //errordetail[1]/message/text()

But this error details will loop when additional information is there.  use string-join(//errordetail/message, ' | ') if error message is like below

 <errordetails>
      <errordetail>
        <ContentID/>
        <code>IWO_BAPI/121</code>
        <message>Error reading the order 34643616436 in the document tables</message>
        <propertyref/>
        <severity>error</severity>
        <target/>
        <transition>true</transition>
      </errordetail>
	  <errordetail>
        <ContentID/>
        <code>IWO_BAPI/121</code>
        <message>check additional info</message>
        <propertyref/>
        <severity>error</severity>
        <target/>
        <transition>true</transition>
      </errordetail>
    </errordetails>

 

output is : Error reading the order 34643616436 in the document tables | check additional info

 

But by seeing your payload, you are getting odata error response from SAP. in help.sap you have groovy to capture this error. Not sure how you defined exception subprocess to handle response

entry.title: How to connect from SAC to Azure SFTP server to load data from a text file
entry.content:
Hi Experts.

How to connect from SAC to Azure SFTP server to load data from a pipe separated values file, into a public dimension. Looks to me this is a most common issue.

Main challenge is to make sure that connection is established to a SFTP server on an Azure cloud server, as it is not standard for SAC.

Later scheduling a job picking up a named file can be done in SAC, like any other connections, after mapping, and transformations etc. 

Thanks in advance,

SP.

entry.title: Re: SAP Service and Asset Manager 2410, DataSubscriptions...
entry.content:
What version of MDK are you using?

Data subscriptions on the action bar were introduced in MDK 24.11.  From the screenshot I see you are running SSAM which did not add support for MDK 24.11.x.   The upcoming SSAM version later this month will introduce support for MDK 25.6.0 so I suspect you will need to wait for that to use Data Subscriptions in the Action Bar with SSAM.

Ratnakar

entry.title: What is the best practice when adding a candidate in RCM and then inviting them to apply?
entry.content:
Hello,

We are trying to use the "CRM" function within RCM and were hoping to add candidates manually when we have their resume.   We would then move them to a requisition or talent pool.  If moving to a req, we would then "Invite to Apply" to ensure they complete an application.

The issue we are experiencing, is it's very cumbersome for a candidate to log into their profile to finish it and complete an application.  Reset Password takes the candidate to a page to enter email and then another page to enter email at which time they will receive an email to reset password.  

Are there any other solutions or "best practices" for this process?  

Thank you,

Corey

entry.title: Re: Implemented OAuth2 Policy in APIM Integration suite s...
entry.content:
I am using the APP key and APP secret of the application from Developer hub which has the apis's in postman to generate the Token.

I have added the Clientid and ClientSecret from the service key of SAP Process Integration instance in  KVM and accessed in the TargetEndPoint and used in Basic Auth policy, But it is still not working, Getting response '401 UnAuthorized'

KVM

<KeyValueMapOperations mapIdentifier="ClientCredentialsA" async="false" continueOnError="false" enabled="true"
    xmlns="http://www.sap.com/apimgmt">
     <Get assignTo="clientid">
        <Key>
            <Parameter>clientid</Parameter>
        </Key>
    </Get>
   <Get assignTo="clientsecret">
        <Key>
            <Parameter>clientsecret</Parameter>
        </Key>
    </Get>
</KeyValueMapOperations>

RemoveAuthorizationHeader

<AssignMessage async="false" continueOnError="false" enabled="false" xmlns="http://www.sap.com/apimgmt">
    <Remove>
        <Headers>
            <Header name="Authorization"/>
        </Headers>
    </Remove>
   <IgnoreUnresolvedVariables>true</IgnoreUnresolvedVariables>
    <AssignTo createNew="false" transport="http" type="request"></AssignTo>
</AssignMessage>

BasicAuthentication.

<BasicAuthentication async='true' continueOnError='false' enabled='true' xmlns='http://www.sap.com/apimgmt'>
	<Operation>Encode</Operation>
	<IgnoreUnresolvedVariables>false</IgnoreUnresolvedVariables>
	<User ref='clientid'></User>
	<Password ref='clientsecret'></Password>
	<AssignTo createNew="false">request.header.Authorization</AssignTo>
</BasicAuthentication>

entry.title: Wrong behaviour of cl_gui_alv_grid or wrong usage?
entry.content:
Hey folks!

I have the following issue when using an F4-value help in cl_gui_alv_grid:

I use a custom F4-value using event onf4.

The input of this field is checked by event data_changed.

When I enter the value manually, the check will be done and documented by add_protocol_entry.

When I select the value by F4-value help, then the check will also be done, but the protocol does not appear.

please see the following sample code.

I will be happy for any feedback.

REPORT.

PARAMETERS test.

CLASS appl DEFINITION.
  PUBLIC SECTION.
    METHODS start IMPORTING container TYPE REF TO cl_gui_container.
  PRIVATE SECTION.
    DATA grid TYPE REF TO cl_gui_alv_grid.
    TYPES: BEGIN OF ts_data,
             id TYPE c LENGTH 10,
           END OF ts_data,
           tt_data TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF ts_data WITH DEFAULT KEY.
    DATA data TYPE tt_data.
    METHODS get_fcat RETURNING VALUE(result) TYPE lvc_t_fcat.
    METHODS on_data_changed FOR EVENT data_changed OF cl_gui_alv_grid IMPORTING e_onf4 e_onf4_after e_onf4_before e_ucomm er_data_changed.
    METHODS on_f4 FOR EVENT onf4 OF cl_gui_alv_grid IMPORTING e_display e_fieldname e_fieldvalue er_event_data es_row_no et_bad_cells.
ENDCLASS.

CLASS appl IMPLEMENTATION.
  METHOD start.

    grid = NEW #( i_parent = container ).
    DATA(lt_fcat) = get_fcat( ).
    data = VALUE #( ( ) ).
    grid->set_table_for_first_display(
      CHANGING
        it_outtab                     = data
        it_fieldcatalog               = lt_fcat
      EXCEPTIONS
        invalid_parameter_combination = 1                    " Wrong Parameter
        program_error                 = 2                    " Program Errors
        too_many_lines                = 3                    " Too many Rows in Ready for Input Grid
        OTHERS                        = 4  ).
    grid->register_edit_event( cl_gui_alv_grid=>mc_evt_modified ).
    grid->set_ready_for_input( 1 ).
    grid->register_f4_for_fields( VALUE #( ( fieldname = 'ID' register = abap_true ) )  ).
    SET HANDLER on_data_changed FOR grid.
    SET HANDLER on_f4 FOR grid.

    cl_gui_container=>set_focus( grid ).

  ENDMETHOD.
  METHOD get_fcat.

    result = VALUE #(
      ( fieldname  = 'ID'
        inttype    = 'C'
        rollname   = 'CHAR10'
        edit       = abap_true
        f4availabl = abap_true
        datatype   = 'CHAR'
        reptext    = 'DEMO' ) ).

  ENDMETHOD.
  METHOD on_data_changed.
    LOOP AT er_data_changed->mt_good_cells ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<cell>).
      CASE <cell>-fieldname.
        WHEN 'ID'.
          IF <cell>-value = 'WRONG'.
            er_data_changed->add_protocol_entry(
                i_msgid     = 'OO'
                i_msgty     = 'E'
                i_msgno     = '000'
                i_msgv1     = 'Input is wrong!'
                i_fieldname = <cell>-fieldname
                i_row_id    = <cell>-row_id
                i_tabix     = <cell>-tabix ).
            IF e_onf4 = 'A'.
              MESSAGE 'Input is wrong!' TYPE 'I'.
            ENDIF.
          ENDIF.
      ENDCASE.
    ENDLOOP.
  ENDMETHOD.
  METHOD on_f4.

    DATA answer TYPE c LENGTH 1.

    CALL FUNCTION 'POPUP_TO_DECIDE'
      EXPORTING
        defaultoption     = '1'          " Cursor position on first or second alternative
        textline1         = 'Please choose'
        text_option1      = 'OKAY'
        text_option2      = 'WRONG'
        icon_text_option1 = 'OKAY'
        icon_text_option2 = 'WRONG'
        titel             = 'Demo'
        start_column      = 25           " Start column of the dialog box
        start_row         = 6            " Start line of the dialog box
        cancel_display    = 'X'
      IMPORTING
        answer            = answer.
    IF answer = 'A'.
      RETURN.
    ENDIF.

    FIELD-SYMBOLS <mod_values> TYPE lvc_t_modi.
    ASSIGN er_event_data->m_data->* TO <mod_values>.
    <mod_values> = VALUE lvc_t_modi(
         ( row_id    = es_row_no-row_id
           fieldname = e_fieldname
           value     = SWITCH #( answer WHEN '1' THEN 'OKAY' ELSE 'WRONG' ) ) ).

    er_event_data->m_event_handled = abap_true.

  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.

INITIALIZATION.
  DATA(docker) = NEW cl_gui_docking_container( side = cl_gui_docking_container=>dock_at_bottom ratio = 90 ).
  NEW appl( )->start( docker ).

Testing

Variant a) Enter "wrong" manually -> protocol entry appears

Variant b) choose value WRONG by F4 -> protocol will not be shown

Hints

on_data_changed-onf4 has value "A" after onf4-event. so I can react on this issue.

er_data_changed->protocol_is_visible() returns "0" in both cases.

But in my opinion this is an error in cl_gui_alv_grid. I do not see any reason why in this case the protocol should be hidden...

entry.title: Issue with Destination Visibility in Cloud Identity Services – Connectivity Plan Required or Workaro
entry.content:
We are currently facing an issue when attempting to create a Source System in the Identity Provisioning Service. Specifically, the destination we've configured in our SAP BTP subaccount isn't appearing as an option in Cloud Identity Services. Based on our research, it seems we may need to create a Cloud Identity Services Connectivity Plan within the same subaccount, as suggested by this community thread: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/no-destination-available-at-identity-provisioning-service/qaq-p/12609309.

We have been following this comprehensive guide to enable Joule capabilities for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition: https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blog-posts-by-sap/joule-for-sap-s-4hana-cloud-private-edition-a-comprehensive-setup-guide/ba-p/13786453.

However, we encountered an error while trying to create this Connectivity Plan, matching the scenario described in SAP Knowledge Base Article 3462424: https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3462424.

Could the absence of this Connectivity Plan indeed be the root cause of our issue, or is there an alternative workaround available that would allow the already-created BTP destination to appear within the Cloud Identity Services? Any insights or guidance from the community would be greatly appreciated.

entry.title: Re: Implemented OAuth2 Policy in APIM Integration suite s...
entry.content:
update KVM like below 

<KeyValueMapOperations mapIdentifier="ClientCredentialsA" async="false" continueOnError="false" enabled="true" xmlns="http://www.sap.com/apimgmt">
	<ExclusiveCache>false</ExclusiveCache>
	<Get assignTo="clientid">
		<Key>
			<Parameter>clientid</Parameter>
		</Key>
	</Get>
	<Get assignTo="clientsecret">
		<Key>
			<Parameter>clientsecret</Parameter>
		</Key>
	</Get>
	<Scope>environment</Scope>
</KeyValueMapOperations>

entry.title: Ready to hire status automatically closing job req
entry.content:
Hi experts,

We are using  SuccessFactors Recruiting and Onboarding.

When a job requisition has “Number of Openings = 1”, and we move the selected applicant to “Ready to Hire”, the job requisition automatically closes. However, this happens before we’ve had the chance to initiate the onboarding process.

As a result, we are forced to enter the closed requisition in order to access it and initiate onboarding.

Is there a way to delay the auto-closure until after onboarding has been initiated, or any best practices to handle this scenario more efficiently?

The “Ready to Hire” status is set to recruitable, which is my understanding of the correct configuration.

All input much appreciated!

entry.title: Configuring Functional Area for Universal Allocations Fiori Apps
entry.content: I would like to configure field RFAREA Functional Area to be a receiver option when using Universal Allocations Fiori Applications. I have changed the configuration in table V_T811I in order for it to be an option when choosing a receiver in the apps. It's now showing up as an option, but I am getting the error "Field use for mandatory field RFAREA is not maintained correctly (T811I)". The only thing I changed was making it an option as a receiver in the "Field usage in allocation" section in that field's entry in T811I. I have tried changing the configuration to match profit center which is a standard option, and that has not worked either. Any suggestions on how field RFAREA Functional Area should be used in order to use it as a receiver in the Fiori Apps?

5,000ソムのクーポンパック + 初回30%割引 + 無料ギフト + シークレットオファー

  5. クーポン、割引、無料ギフトを楽しむ

📌まとめ
• コード: alf145955
• コードの種類: プロモコード、クーポンコード、割引コード、招待コード
• 特典: 5,000ソムクーポン、30%割引、無料ギフト
• 対象: 新規と再登録ユーザー
• 対応地域: キルギスと他の国

entry.title: Adhoc notifications triggered manually not received by users in LMS
entry.content:
Hi, Everyone!

I am having an issue with the LMS sending certain email notifications when triggered manually. When I navigate to a class and select Actions > Resend Registration Email Notifications, the emails are delivered from the LMS to the users just fine. However, when I navigate to Manage User Learning > Send Email Notifications, and follow the steps to specify the criteria type, the program ID, the population,  and build out my email, it will not send the email to the users.

I ran an audit report, and it shows that the email was "sent," but nothing has been received. I even sent a preview of the email from the "Launch Editor" window, and that email was received, but the actual email that was sent to my test users (which included me) was not received.

From my audit report, I noticed that the preview email and the emails to our users came from different addresses. The preview email came from the email address that is listed as our "Application Notification Admin Email" in the LMS, and it was delivered. The emails that were sent to our test users came from my personal work email address, and it was not delivered. The LMS will not allow me to change the "From" email from my work email to the Application Notification Admin Email. Does someone have an answer to this, because it's strange that it won't send even if it is from my work email address?

entry.title: Тему жеңілдік коды Қазақстан – alf145955
entry.content:
Тему жеңілдік коды Қазақстан – alf145955
45 000 ₸ купон пакеті, 30% алғашқы тапсырысқа жеңілдік, тегін өнім және эксклюзивті ұсыныстар.

Егер сіз Тему қосымшасын Қазақстанда алғаш рет жүктеп жатсаңыз немесе бұрын орнатып қайта тіркелсеңіз — ең тиімді жеңілдіктерге жол ашылды!

alf145955 промокоды арқылы артықшылықтар:
• 45 000 ₸ көлеміндегі купондар
• Алғашқы тапсырысыңызға 30% жеңілдік
• 0 ₸ тегін өнім таңдау мүмкіндігі
• Тек шақыру арқылы қолжетімді арнайы жеңілдіктер

alf145955 шақыру коды тек Қазақстанда ғана емес, Тему қолжетімді басқа елдерде де жарамды.
Бұл код жасырын жеңілдіктерді, үлкен купон пакеттерін және тегін өнімдерді ашуға мүмкіндік береді.
Код жаңа қолданушыларға да, қайта тіркелген пайдаланушыларға да жарамды.

Артықшылықтар тізімі:
• 45 000 ₸ купон пакеті — тіркелген соң купондар автоматты түрде шотыңызға қосылады
• 30% алғашқы тапсырысқа жеңілдік (минимум тапсырыс 6 000 ₸, максимум жеңілдік 13 500 ₸)
• Тегін өнім (0 ₸) — жаңа қолданушылар тегін өнім таңдап, тегін жеткізумен ала алады
• Эксклюзивті ұсыныстар — тек шақыру арқылы қолжетімді

Қазақстанда Тему ұсыныстарын іске қосу жолы:

  1. Жаңа тіркелгі жасаңыз немесе қосымшаны қайта орнатып қайта тіркеліңіз

  2. Өнімдерді себетке қосыңыз

  3. Төлем бетінде alf145955 жеңілдік кодын енгізіңіз

  4. Купондардан, 30% жеңілдіктен және тегін өнімнен ләззат алыңыз

Неліктен Тему-ды Қазақстанда таңдау керек?
Тему — сән, электроника, тұрмыстық заттар мен сыйлықтарға дейінгі кең ассортиментті төмен бағамен және сенімді жеткізіліммен ұсынады.
alf145955 жеңілдік коды арқылы алғашқы тапсырысыңызды тиімді жолмен бастайсыз.

Қысқаша шолу:
• Код: alf145955
• Артықшылықтар: 45 000 ₸ купон пакеті, 30% жеңілдік, тегін өнім
• Қолданушылар: жаңа және қайта тіркелген
• Қолдану аймағы: Қазақстан және басқа елдер

Бүгін alf145955 кодын қолданыңыз және Тему артықшылықтарын пайдаланыңыз!

entry.title: Тэму арзандатуу коду Кыргызстан – alf145955
entry.content:
Тэму арзандатуу коду Кыргызстан – alf145955
5 000 сом купон топтому, 30% арзандатуу, бекер белек жана жашыруун сунуштар.

Эгер Тэму тиркемесин биринчи жолу жүктөп жатсаңыз же кайра орнотуп кайра катталсаңыз — alf145955 чакыруу коду сизге эң жогорку деңгээлдеги артыкчылыктарды берет:
• 5 000 сом көлөмүндөгү купон пакети
• Биринчи буйрутмага 30% арзандатуу
• 0 сом менен бекер продукт (жеткирүү менен)
• Жашыруун арзандатуудагы товарларга жетүү мүмкүнчүлүгү

alf145955 чакыруу коду Кыргызстанда эле эмес, Тэму жеткиликтүү болгон башка өлкөлөрдө да иштейт.
Бул код эң жогорку деңгээлдеги купон топтомдорун, жашыруун арзандатуулардын кулпусун ачат жана бекер белек алууга мүмкүнчүлүк берет.
Бул арзандатуу коду жаңы колдонуучуларга да, тиркемени кайра орнотуп кайра катталган колдонуучуларга да жарактуу.

Эмне аласыз?
• 5 000 сом купон пакети — катталгандан кийин автоматтык түрдө активделет
• 30% арзандатуу биринчи буйрукка (минималдуу буйрутма суммасы 700 сом, максималдуу арзандатуу 2 000 сом)
• Бекер белек (0 сом) — бир продукцияны тандоо жана жеткирүү бекер
• Жашыруун сунуштар — чакыруу аркылуу гана көрүүгө мүмкүн болгон атайын арзандатылган товарлар

Тэму артыкчылыктарын Кыргызстанда кантип колдонсо болот?

  1. Жаңы аккаунт түзүңүз же колдонмону кайра орнотуп кайра катталыңыз

  2. Сүйүктүү товарларды себетке кошуңуз

  3. Төлөм экранында alf145955 арзандатуу кодун киргизиңиз

  4. Купондордон, арзандатуудан жана бекер белектен ырахат алыңыз

Кыскача маалымат:
• Активдүү код: alf145955
• Артыкчылыктар: 5 000 сом купон, 30% арзандатуу, бекер белек
• Ким үчүн: жаңы жана кайра катталган колдонуучулар
• Жарактуу аймактар: Кыргызстан жана башка өлкөлөр

Бүгүн alf145955 кодун колдонуп, Тэмудагы мыкты сунуштарга ээ болуңуз!

entry.title: New Machine Learning and AI features in SAP HANA Cloud 2025 Q2
entry.content:
With the SAP HANA Cloud 2025 Q2 release, several new embedded Machine Learning / AI functions have been released with the SAP HANA Cloud Predictive Analysis Library (PAL) and the Automated Predictive Library (APL).

Key new capabilities to be highlighted include

  • A new text embedding model version, covering more languages and short-text embedding scenarios,
  • more tabular AI functions enhanced to support vector data processing like AutoML, k-nearest neighbors (k-NN),
  • outlier detection using isolation forest supporting outlier explainability
  • machine learning experiment tracking and task scheduling capabilities
  • data drift analysis using the Automated Predictive Library

An enhancement summary is available in the What’s new document for SAP HANA Cloud database 2025.14 (QRC 2/2025).

Text processing, text embedding and vector processing using ML functions

Text tokenization

A new text tokenization function has been released, allowing to split text into tokens, a fundamental preparation step in natural language processing (NLP) like text analysis, and many other downstream text processing tasks like text embedding or vector similarity search. The new function support keystokenization capabilities like

  • removal of common stopwords (e.g., "the", "and", "is").
  • filtering out purely numeric and alphanumeric tokens
  • specifications of characters or symbols that should always be kept or removed
  • controls for stemming, language detection.

 

Text embedding

A new text embedding model version (SAP_GXY.20250407) is made available, which has been fine-tuned based on a roberta-base-encoder model and more training data for improved retrieval accuracy, short text scenarios and extend multi-lingual & cross-lingual retrieval scenarios. New additional languages supported include Chinese (CH), Japanese (JP) and Italian (IT). The default token length for embedding functions has been increase to 512.

Extended embedding vector processing by Machine Learning functions

Unlocking the semantic understanding of your text data stored in SAP HANA Cloud, for use cases like similarity search, however moreover for machine learning scenarios like  document/text- classification and –clustering, and more has now been extended to even more PAL Machine Learning functions

  • K-nearest neighbor models (K-NN) for classification/regression/similarity search
  • Random Decision Trees for classification/regression models
  • AutoML scenarios for classification, regression and times series
    • including the fit, prediction, scoring, and pipeline procedures
    • support with all pipeline operators (algorithms), except for the Imputer and
       ImputeTS-operators

 

Machine Learning function enhancements

Outlier detection enhancements using Isolation Forests

The Isolation Forest  function for outlier detection has been enhanced with

  • support categorial columns as features in outlier models
  • a new massive outlier detection function for parallel analysis of multiple data subsets
    (PAL_MASSIVE_ISOLATION_FOREST)
  • a new outlier explanation method based on Shapley values (PAL_ISOLATION_FOREST_EXPLAIN)
    • providing local explanations for the predicted outlier classification,
    • describing the contribution of each feature to the predicted classification,
      based on a Tree-SHAP explainer model,
    • options to configure the explanation function by setting contamination level (proportion of outliers), scope (explanations for outliers only or all predictions), top K contributions (set k number of features in explanations)

Isolation Forest is a strong and trending function for outlier detection, which can be applied on any data for outlier analysis inside the database, hence especially suitable also for use cases where data shall not leave the system or is too big to be copied out for analysis like use cases for detecting outliers on your financial accounting, like the universal journal data (ACDOCA).

 

Constraint Clustering

A new constraint clustering function is introduced, an advanced form of clustering that incorporates domain-specific constraints or prior information to guide the clustering process, ensuring more accurate and meaningful results tailored to specific analytical needs. Traditional clustering methods are mostly limited and cannot include prior knowledge about the data, often leading to challenges in achieving meaningful or contextually relevant groupings.

Prior contexts can be included in the clustering process as

  • Pairwise constraints: Must-link / Must-Not-link constraints of data points to be / not in the same cluster
  • Triplet Constraints: With an anchor instance (a), a positive instance (p), and a negative instance (n), the constraint shows that instance a is more similar to p than to n.

A detailed introduction to the new function is given in the following blog post https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/clustering-text-documents-using-constrained-clustering/ba-p/14134157

 

Further enhanced machine learning algorithms for Tabular AI scenarios

The recently implemented Multi-task MLP (multi-layer perceptron) neural network function, unlocking predictions for multiple targets / labels using a single model, has now been enhanced with an improved built-in model evaluation and parameter search interface, providing faster approach and productivity to achieve even better prediction outcomes.

The new optimization can be leveraged by calling the function directly or via its use within the Unified Classification/Regression functions and supports to search and select the following optimal neural network model parameter values

  • LEARNING_RATE, EMBEDDED_NUM, RESIDUAL_NUM, DROPOUT_PROB, HIDDEN_LAYER_SIZE, HIDDEN_LAYER_ACTIVE_FUNC, OPTIMIZER

 

In the domain of time series analysis and forecasting, the ARIMA forecasting function now supports to keep context of the time horizon index and interval

  • Trained ARIMA model keeps track of the final time index value and the determined index interval
  • Subsequently, ARIMA_PREDICT will use this information to output forecast values with a continuous index

 

Machine Learning experiment tracking and task scheduling

Experiment tracking and monitoring for PAL ML models

Machine learning experimentation requires robust tracking capabilities to ensure reproducibility, comparison, and auditability of models. SAP HANA Cloud's new ML tracking feature provides seamless integration with Predictive Analysis Library (PAL) procedures, enabling automatic logging of critical experiment artifacts. This end-to-end tracking solution captures parameters, datasets, models, metrics, and visualizations in a structured way, transforming how data scientists manage ML workflows.

The new execution tracking of PAL procedure supports

  • use with al Unified Regression/Classification-, Pipeline-fit/score procedures (with new interfaces suffix  “_TRACK” suffix)
  • tracking of content including: Parameters, Dataset Metadata, Model Signature, Metrics(including Variable Importance), Figures(discrete and continuous), etc.
  • management of tracking data in a built-in schema PAL_ML_TRACK and tables for metadata, log and header information
  • procedure parameters activate the tracking like LOG_ML_TRACK, TRACK_ID, etc.

A more detailed introduction is provided in the following blog post https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/comprehensive-guide-to-mltrack-in-sap-hana-cloud-end-to-end-machine/ba-p/14134217

 

Task scheduling for PAL procedures

A new PAL task scheduling allows you to run SQLScript procedures (calling PAL procedures) by the SAP HANA Cloud schedular asynchronously (cron-based). The targeted SQLScript (PAL) procedure calls get mapped to a define task with task ID, task descriptions, owner, etc. A task can be scheduled to executed, a job is the instance of scheduled task.

  • The built-in schema PAL_SCHEDULED_EXECUTION provides tables for storage of task definition/metadata, relationships, procedures parameters, TASK_SCHEDULE_JOB, task (operation) log
  • The actual job execution information is provided within the System views SCHEDULE_JOBS, M_SCHEDULE_JOBS
  • New PAL procedures are provided for task creation (AFLPAL_CREATE_TASK_PROC)
    and removal, scheduled job creation for a task (AFLPAL_CREATE_TASK_SCHEDULE_PROC), altering, pausing, resuming and removing task jobs

The python ML client adds additional interfaces and methods to leverage the new capabilities easily by experts developing HANA ML scenarios.

 

Python ML client (hana-ml) enhancements

The full list of new methods and enhancements with hana_ml 2.25  is summarized in the changelog for hana-ml 2.25 as part of the documentation. The key enhancements in this release include

Text and vector processing enhancements

  • New embedding model version SAP_GXY.20250407

Classification / regression function enhancements

  • Use of Multi-task MLP with UnifiedClassification|Regression
    • Benefit from unified interface capabilities like score-function, resampling/parameter search and optimization, model-report, …
  • HGBT regressor with Linear Tree trend extrapolation

AutoML and pipeline modeling improvements

  • Faster random search with hyperband optimization support
  • Progress monitor enhancements for fine-tuning and random search
  • HANA ML experiment tracking and task schedule execution
  • Experiment tracking and experiment monitor UI (detailed introduction is provided in the referenced blog post)
  • Enhanced PAL task schedule and job chedule execution UI

You can find an examples notebook illustrating the highlighted feature enhancements here 25QRC02_2.25.ipynb.

entry.title: Re: HCM Brasil: Crédito do Trabalhador - eConsignado - Atualizações
entry.content:
Boa tarde.

Surgiu uma situação que não está sendo tratada pelo SAP.  Quando o funcionário tem mais de um empréstimo (contratos diferentes) no mesmo banco, com parcelas no mesmo período lançados no IT3453. No calculo da folha a função BRPID soma os descontos gerando apenas uma rubrica MCT0. Na geração do evento S-1200 vai a rubrica MCT0 (com o valor total) mas referencia apenas um dos contratos registrados, e pela norma do emprestimo consignado todos as parcelas descontadas com seus respectivos contratos devem ser informados.

entry.title: Re: Cannot Reverse Good Receipt from Vendor after Putaway...
entry.content:
Good Morning,

Unfortunatelly after a putaway Warehouse Task confirmation you are not able to perform goods recept reversal. You can refer to SAP Note 3455535

https://me.sap.com/notes/3455535/E

As it is mentioned in point 1 of reversal of goods receipt if a putaway status is completed GRR is not possible.

  • Reversal of goods receipt:

You can reverse the goods receipt of the stock through app "Change Inbound Deliveries - Deliveries". If any of the following restrictions apply to one of the items, the GRR will not be possible:

1. Putaway status is already completed

2. There is an open Warehouse Task for the item

3. Item is QI relevant and Quality decision is already made

4. Stock content was changed, e.g. goods receipt was posted using a handling unit and this handling unit has been deleted or stock type was changed with a posting change

In such cases you should create a Return Purchase Order and return delivery in reference to this Purchase Order. 

  • Creation of return delivery:

If the putaway WT was already confirmed you need to create a return purchase order. With this you can create an outbound delivery based on this return PO, pick and goods issue the stock.
Follow the steps described in scope item "Return to Supplier (BMK)".

If you have any other question please let me know.

Best Regards

Dariusz Kalmarczyk

entry.title: Re: Adhoc notifications triggered manually not received b...
entry.content: @Ivy_McGruder, the steps outlined in KBA 2328647 should assist you in modifying the "From" email. If emails are being sent from Learning (as indicated in the email audit report), they may be getting bounced or rejected by the receiving email server. It's advisable to review the information provided in KBA 2087468 in collaboration with the IT team.

entry.title: Re: EDI Setup for Billing Document - S4 HANA Public Cloud

Hi Asesh,

Yes, EDI for Billing Documents is available in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, but the setup runs through Output Parameter Determination (OPD) with BRF+

Useful reference Links:

  • SAP Help Portal – Output Management (S/4HANA Cloud)
  • SAP Help Portal – Output Parameter Determination Overview
  • SAP KBA 2874561 – Output Management for Billing Documents in S/4HANA Cloud (SAP login required)

With kind regards

Chuma

entry.title: Inconsistencies in SAP Cloud ALM Test execution Analysis
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

Currently I have been using SAP Cloud ALM for testing management purposes.

We have massively loaded test cases & distribute them into their respective test plans. The status for these test plans is set to "In testing" meaning that they are already available for execution in the "Test execution" app.

When entering the "Test execution" app, and looking at the pool of tests with status "initial", I get a number of 5451, which is correct, shown as follow:

 However, when clicking on the "Analisis" button (that jumps you to the Analysis app), the number of test cases with status initial, does not correspond with the correct one shown above for these exact same test plans.

 When analyzing in the Analysis app, I encounter that, for 4 out of 6 test plans, the result of test cases with status "Initial" is only one, which is not correct.

 

 

Any thoughts on how to proceed?

Or is there anything I could possibly be doing wrong?

Thanks in advance!

Sebastián

entry.title: Event management architecture
entry.content:
Hi Experts- Need your help. My client's requirement is to track the Shipments status realtime across all multi model shipments with mutiple countries.  They Have TM implemented already & the current requirement is to implement EM & a 3rd party application to get the shipment status updates (RFID/GPS/GPRS) at each leg of the shipment process. 

The question I have related to "TM-EM- 3rd party application" architecture is : 

1. How about the 3rd party application which sends & receives the events (RFID/GPS/GPRS status)?. Will that 3rd party application will be connected to EM & EM will feed the updates to TM? 

or 

2. Will that 3rd party application be directly connected to TM? & TM send the updates to EM? 

Please advise which architecture is best feasible solution.

Yes. As far as I'm aware of, the classifications Featured, New, and Revised are only visible as a filter (in the Flagged section) through Enhanced Search. That is, these values are not displayed anywhere else.

entry.title: Re: Background jobs for vendor and document changes in fiori
entry.content:
Hello @Shama321 

Thank you for your question. In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, transactions like S_ALR_87012089 (Vendor Changes) and S_ALR_87012293 (Document Changes) are generally not available, because the Public Cloud restricts classic GUI transactions

Refer to the SAP Help documentation below

  • Schedule Application Jobs → App ID: F1680.SAP Help Portal – Application Job Scheduling (S/4HANA Cloud)
  • SAP Help Portal – Configuration to Schedule an Application Job (S/4HANA Cloud)
  •  Some reporting gaps may be addressed in future Public Cloud releases. You can check the SAP Roadmap Explorer

Execute in Background for classic ALR reports like Vendor Changes and Document Changes is not enabled in Public Cloud

With kind regards

Chuma

entry.title: SLD does not update the company list even though the SRGC table is correct
entry.content:
Hi, I'm facing a persistent issue with the System Landscape Directory (SLD) in SAP Business One.

When I log into the SAP B1 client and click on "Change Company", then hit "Refresh", the list of companies either disappears or shows outdated information. If I switch between the available SQL servers (SQL 2012 and SQL 2014), the list reappears, but it does not reflect recent changes (e.g., deleted databases still appear or new ones are missing).

I've already checked the SRGC table in the SBO-COMMON database, and it contains the correct and up-to-date company information. However, the SLD still shows outdated entries.

I also accessed the SLD through the web browser, and the issue is the same: it does not show the actual list of available databases.

I'm considering removing and re-registering the SQL server in the SLD, but I'm unsure whether this might affect any configuration or deployment.

Has anyone experienced this issue?

  • What file or process does SAP use to rebuild the company list from the SQL instance?

  • Is it safe to remove and re-add the server in the SLD?

  • How can I force the SLD to resync properly with SBO-COMMON?

Any guidance would be greatly appreciated.
